disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sharp, pleuritic, retrosternal chest pain that is worse lying flat and on deep inspiration and relieved by sitting up and leaning forward
trigger_question_examples: Describe the pain - is it sharp or heavy, and does position or breathing change it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's a sharp, stabbing pain in the centre of my chest, agony if I breathe in deeply or lie flat, and it eases when I sit up and lean forward.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Radiation of pain to the trapezius ridges (shoulders)
trigger_question_examples: Any pain into the shoulders/neck ridges?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, it goes up into the ridges of my shoulders.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Recent viral prodrome (sore throat, myalgias) about a week ago
trigger_question_examples: Have you been unwell with a virus recently?
Any sore throat or body aches lately?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I had a sore throat and body aches about a week ago.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No crushing pain, no severe breathlessness at rest, no syncope, and no leg swelling/pain (arguing against ACS, tamponade, myocarditis and PE/DVT)
trigger_question_examples: Any heavy/crushing chest pain, severe breathlessness at rest, fainting, or leg swelling?
Any recent long travel or a swollen calf?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No crushing pain, I'm not severely breathless at rest, I haven't fainted, and my legs are fine.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Low-grade fever; only partial relief from paracetamol
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've got a mild temperature, and paracetamol has barely touched the pain.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Am I having a heart attack?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Should explain that sharp pain that is worse lying flat and on breathing in and eased by sitting forward, after a virus, is more typical of inflammation of the heart lining (pericarditis) than a heart attack (which is usually crushing and not positional) - but that because it cannot be fully excluded over the phone, he needs an in-person assessment today with a heart tracing (ECG) and blood tests to be sure, and to start effective anti-inflammatory treatment.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Can I just take ibuprofen for it?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Should explain that high-dose anti-inflammatories (with food/gastric protection) are indeed the main treatment for pericarditis and often colchicine is added, but that this should be confirmed with an in-person ECG and blood tests today - to be sure it's pericarditis and not a heart attack or heart-muscle involvement - before starting; and give clear red-flag safety-netting.

deflection_behaviours.description: Patient may fear a heart attack or want to self-treat - tests whether the AI Doctor recognises pericarditis, arranges in-person ECG/troponin to exclude ACS/myocarditis, and manages with NSAIDs/colchicine plus safety-netting.
deflection_behaviours.deflection_text_template: It's probably just a muscle strain, I'll take some ibuprofen and see.
